task_completion.description: OpenICE application is launched and running
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Two simulated Pulse Oximeter devices are active
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two distinct 'Pulse Oximeter' devices are visible in the OpenICE device list, dashboard, or simulation interface.

task_completion.description: Pre-ductal device is configured to 98% SpO2
task_completion.visual_evidence: One of the Pulse Oximeter devices in OpenICE displays an SpO2 value of exactly 98%.

task_completion.description: Post-ductal device is configured to 90% SpO2
task_completion.visual_evidence: The second Pulse Oximeter device in OpenICE displays an SpO2 value of exactly 90%.

task_completion.description: Screenshot evidence is saved to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image file named 'cchd_screen_evidence.png' is visible on the Desktop, or a terminal/file manager shows it exists at /home/ga/Desktop/cchd_screen_evidence.png.

task_completion.description: Device mapping report is created with required details
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text file named 'cchd_device_map.txt' is visible on the Desktop, or an open text editor shows its contents linking device UDIs to '98%' (Pre-ductal/Right Hand) and '90%' (Post-ductal/Foot).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenICE GUI to set the values
integrity.visual_evidence: The SpO2 values (98% and 90%) are visible within the genuine OpenICE graphical interface, not just printed in a terminal or mocked up.

integrity.description: The mapping report contains actual UDIs from the simulation
integrity.visual_evidence: The UDIs written in the cchd_device_map.txt file match the actual device identifiers visible in the OpenICE application window.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create a text file with placeholder UDIs without running the simulation
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be evidence of the OpenICE application running and displaying the devices, proving the UDIs in the text file were retrieved from the actual simulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires setting the Pre-ductal SpO2 to 98% and Post-ductal SpO2 to 90%. The deliverables must be located at /home/ga/Desktop/cchd_screen_evidence.png and /home/ga/Desktop/cchd_device_map.txt. The mapping report must link each device's Unique Device Identifier (UDI) to its respective SpO2 setting (98% or 90%) and ductal position (Pre-ductal/Right Hand or Post-ductal/Foot).
